fixture.prompt: Turn this rough intent into acceptance criteria, edge cases, validation, rollback, and a handoff another engineer can execute.
fixture.rationale: Executable acceptance criteria and handoff artifact.

baseline_invocation.input: Turn this rough intent into acceptance criteria, edge cases, validation, rollback, and a handoff another engineer can execute.

candidate_invocation.input: Turn this rough intent into acceptance criteria, edge cases, validation, rollback, and a handoff another engineer can execute.

mechanical_port.allowed_difference: Canonical GStack 2 carve: exclude the retired shared onboarding wrapper and host hook advisory; resolve retired invocations to six public routes; relocate host/runtime paths; lazy-load pinned carved sections from package-local references.

differences.explanation:
### User-language rule

Write questions, progress updates, reports, and artifacts in the language used by the user. Source material, code identifiers, commands, and quotations may remain in their original language when translating them would reduce accuracy.

questions.evidence: The candidate exactly matches the deterministic canonical specialist render of the pinned workflow. The retired shared onboarding wrapper is excluded and carved specialist phases are package-local lazy references; specialist questions, pressure, gates, evidence, artifacts, mutation boundaries, exit behavior, and voice remain governed by their source contracts.
